title: Cairn primitive types
description: Shared assertion-bearing primitives referenced across envelope/prelude/verb files. Keeping these in one place prevents identifier and crypto patterns from drifting between call sites.

Ulid.description: Crockford base32 ULID — 26 characters, upper case, 48-bit ms timestamp + 80-bit entropy.

Nonce16Base64.description: 16 random bytes, base64-encoded. 22 significant chars + optional `==` padding; the 22nd significant char is constrained to [AQgw] so the trailing 4 bits are zero — this is the unique canonical encoding of exactly 16 bytes, rejecting non-canonical variants that strict decoders would refuse.

Ed25519Signature.description: Ed25519 signature: 64 raw bytes rendered as 128 lowercase hex chars after the `ed25519:` tag.

Identity.description: AgentIdentity, HumanIdentity, or SensorIdentity. Examples: agt:claude-code:opus-4-7:reviewer:v1, usr:alice, snr:local:screen:host:v1.

Cursor.description: Opaque continuation token. Shared by every request cursor input and every response next_cursor so the pagination round-trip is validator-symmetric — a server cannot emit a cursor the next request cannot carry.
